commands.review_summary:
The PR adds experimental channel-scoped QMD memory search configuration, a Slack route scope resolver, a model-facing `memory_search.scopeOverride`, QMD collection filtering, and related config/schema/tests.

PR surface: Source +314, Tests +234. Total +548 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes from source inspection: enabling `memory.qmd.channelScopes` with normal QMD-managed collections produces requested names that are filtered out before search; I did not run tests because this was a read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human security/product review is needed for the cross-channel memory policy, and the external draft also needs contributor proof and a clean rebase before any repair lane would be safe.

target.title: Add channel-scoped memory search filters
target.body:
# Channel-Scoped Memory PR Report - 2026-06-02

## Status

Implemented directly in the parent session after the Codex native subagent failed before file access due:

`Command blocked by PreToolUse hook: Native hook relay unavailable.`

## Branch

`exp/channel-scoped-memory`

## Commit

`a3c1376d39`

## Summary

- Added experimental `memory.qmd.channelScopes` config for route-derived QMD collection filtering.
- Added a reusable memory scope resolver for Slack channel/DM routes.
- Added `memory_search.scopeOverride` for explicit, reason-gated manual collection expansion.
- Passed scoped collection names through `memory_search` into `MemorySearchManager.search`.
- Updated QMD manager to intersect requested collection names with managed collections before querying.
- Added config schema/types/help/labels for the new channel scope settings.
- Added focused tests for resolver behavior, tool wiring, override denial, and config schema acceptance.

## Milvus Note

This PR does not install or operate Milvus. It creates the policy boundary and collection-filtering shape that a Milvus-backed memory adapter should honor. Milvus deployment remains infrastructure work and should not bypass the same default route scopes and manual override rules.

## Tests Run

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/channel-memory-scope.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts`
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `git diff --check`

## Verification Notes

- A repo-wide `pnpm -s exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false` was attempted first and hit Node heap OOM at about 4 GB. The narrower repo-standard `tsgo` lanes above passed.
- The first Vitest attempt used the wrong unit lane and found no tests; subsequent extension/config lanes passed.

## Files Touched

- `extensions/memory-core/index.ts`
- `extensions/memory-core/src/channel-memory-scope.ts`
- `extensions/memory-core/src/channel-memory-scope.test.ts`
- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory-tool-manager-mock.ts`
- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts`
- `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.shared.ts`
- `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts`
- `extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts`
- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/config-utils.ts`
- `packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/types.ts`
- `src/config/config.schema-regressions.test.ts`
- `src/config/schema.help.ts`
- `src/config/schema.labels.ts`
- `src/config/types.memory.ts`
- `src/config/zod-schema.ts`
